UCL
1
UCL
Real Madrid leads the all-time Champions League titles with 15. The 2nd most successful club has 7 titles. Which team is it?
ALiverpool
BBayern Munich
CMilan
DBarcelona
→ Milan (7 titles)
Milan won in 1963, 1969, 1989, 1990, 1994, 2003 and 2007. Bayern Munich and Liverpool are joint 3rd with 6 titles each.
2
UCL
In 70 editions of the Champions League, Real Madrid leads with 44 appearances. Which club is 2nd?
AAjax
BBenfica
CBayern Munich
DJuventus
→ Benfica
Benfica won back-to-back in 1961 and 1962. Despite many appearances they have not won it since, making them one of European football's great nearly-men.
3
UCL
One player holds the record for most minutes ever played in the Champions League, with 16,267 minutes across 181 matches. Who is this legend?
ACasillas
BCristiano Ronaldo
CNeuer
DMessi
→ Iker Casillas (181 UCL matches)
Casillas played 177 of those 181 UCL games for Real Madrid alone. He won 3 UCL titles with Madrid and is Spain's all-time most capped goalkeeper.
4
UCL
In the 2004/05 UCL final, Liverpool came back from 3-0 down at half-time to win on penalties. One player is hidden in the lineup: he scored 2 goals in the first half for Milan. Who is he?
→ Hernan Crespo
Crespo put Milan 3-0 up but Liverpool came back through Gerrard, Smicer and Alonso. Dudek double save from Shevchenko sealed one of the greatest nights in football history.
International
5
International
Brazil (22), Germany (20), Argentina (18) and Italy (18) lead World Cup appearances. Which nation is 5th with 17?
AFrance
BMexico
CEngland
DUruguay
→ Mexico (17 participations)
Mexico reached the Round of 16 in 8 consecutive World Cups (1994-2018). They have never gone beyond the quarter-finals.
6
International
The World Cup has been held 22 times. How many different nations have lifted the trophy?
A4
B6
C8
D10
→ 8 nations
The 8 winners: Brazil (5), Germany (4), Italy (4), Argentina (3), France (2), Uruguay (2), England (1) and Spain (1). Every winner came from Europe or South America.
7
International
Which team shares the record for worst all-time World Cup Goal Difference with Mexico at -39?
AUnited States
BSwitzerland
CSaudi Arabia
DSouth Korea
→ South Korea (-39)
Despite reaching the semi-finals in 2002 as co-hosts, South Korea's overall World Cup goal difference tells a very different story.
8
International
Germany beat Brazil 7-1 in the 2014 semi-final (the Mineirazo). Brazil scored a consolation in the 90th minute. Who scored it?
AOscar
BWillian
CHulk
DDavid Luiz
→ Oscar
Germany scored 4 goals in 6 minutes between the 69th and 74th. Brazil were without Neymar (injured) and Thiago Silva (suspended).
FPL
9
FPL
Last season Salah broke the all-time FPL season record with 344 points. Who finished 2nd with 236 points and 29 goal involvements?
APalmer
BMbeumo
CIsak
DWood
→ Bryan Mbeumo (236 pts)
Mbeumo scored 20 Premier League goals in 2024/25, carrying Brentford almost single-handedly. One of the most efficient FPL assets despite playing for a mid-table club.
10
FPL
The FPL all-time Dream Team features the best season ever per position. One striker is missing from this picture, he finished with 295 FPL points. Who is it?
AKane
BWatkins
CSuarez
DLukaku
→ Luis Suarez (295 pts)
Suarez 2013/14 at Liverpool: 31 goals in 33 appearances, all from open play. Arguably the finest individual Premier League campaign ever.
11
FPL
This season only 3 FPL players are above 200 points. Can you name all three?
→ Salah (344), Mbeumo (236), Haaland (~210)
12
FPL
Only 5 players have finished as top FPL scorer more than once: Salah (4x), Lampard and Henry (3x each), Cristiano Ronaldo and one other (2x each). Who is the fifth?
AHazard
BVan Persie
CSuarez
DHaaland
→ Robin van Persie (2 times)
Van Persie top-scored at both Arsenal and Manchester United. His 2012/13 season at United (26 league goals) fired them to the title in Ferguson's final year.
Portugal
13
Portugal
Portugal reached the 2006 World Cup semi-finals. Which Portuguese player led the team in goal involvements at that tournament?
ADeco
BFigo
CCristiano Ronaldo
DPauleta
→ Luis Figo
The 2006 World Cup was Figo's international farewell. Portugal finished 4th, a fitting send-off for one of the greatest players of his generation.
14
Portugal
Portugal made their World Cup debut in 1966. How many times have they participated in total, not counting 2026?
A6
B8
C10
D12
→ 8 participations
Portugal missed out for long stretches before becoming regulars from 2002 onwards. The golden generations around Figo and Ronaldo drove that consistency.
Czechia
15
Czechia
Petr Cech holds the Czech Republic record for international appearances with 124 caps. Who is 2nd?
ANedved
BRosicky
CPoborsky
DBaros
→ Karel Poborsky
Poborsky became famous for his lob against Portugal at Euro 96. He won the Champions League with Manchester United as part of the 1999 treble squad.
16
Czechia
The FIFA World Cup started in 1930. In which edition did Czechoslovakia make their debut?
A1930
B1934
C1938
D1950
→ 1934
Czechoslovakia reached the final in their debut World Cup in 1934, losing 2-1 to Italy in extra time. One of the most remarkable debut performances ever.
Random
17
Random
The player in this picture started his senior career at 16 years old. Over 20 seasons he played for 11 clubs across 6 countries. Who is he?
→ Nicolas Anelka
Anelka won league titles in France, England, Turkey and Italy. He won the Champions League with Real Madrid in 2000 and the Premier League with Chelsea in 2010.
18
Random
Only one father and son pair have both won the Champions League. Who are they?
→ Cesare Maldini (1963) and Paolo Maldini (1989, 1990, 1994, 2003, 2007)
Paolo won it 5 times to his father's 1. Both played their entire careers at AC Milan. Paolo never received a single red card in his professional career.
19
Random
Clubs rarely pay fees for managers but it happens. Who holds the record for the largest manager compensation fee ever paid?
AEnzo Maresca (Leicester to Chelsea)
BKompany (Burnley to Bayern)
CThomas Frank (Brentford to Spurs)
DNagelsmann (Leipzig to Bayern)
→ Julian Nagelsmann, approx 25M EUR paid by Bayern Munich to RB Leipzig in 2021
Two Portuguese managers appear in the top 10: Ruben Amorim features twice, for his moves from Sporting CP to Manchester United and a previous appointment.
20
Random
A legend, now a football manager. From the picture showing his club career, can you guess who he is?
→ Patrick Vieira
Vieira won 3 Premier League titles with Arsenal's Invincibles and the 1998 World Cup with France. He has managed New York City FC, Nice, Crystal Palace and Strasbourg.
Bonus
21
Bonus
Name the 5th player in the list, the top 5 players with most goal contributions in the Premier League since 2019/20.
→ Ollie Watkins (126 goal contributions)
Watkins has been one of the most consistent Premier League strikers since 2019/20, combining goals and assists at Brentford and Aston Villa.
